pattern: Same DP as the Easy variant but values reach 5×10^4, requiring a bitset (Python int as bitmask) to keep the prefix-sum DP efficient enough.
hints: The DP idea from the Easy variant still applies: sort and deduplicate, then for each value v mark every reachable total x+v where x < v. The problem is doing this fast enough for large value ranges.

Represent the set of reachable totals as a Python integer used as a bitmask. For value v, the bits below v are candidates; shifting the masked portion left by v and OR-ing it in performs all updates in O(1) bit operations.
The answer is the position of the highest set bit in the bitmask after processing all values. Python arbitrary-precision integers make this bitset approach O(m^2 / 64) in practice.

edge_inputs.description: many duplicates of a large value
values that require skipping one to pick two

stress.description: random array length up to 1000 values up to 2000 (moderate to keep oracle fast)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    vals = [rng.randint(1, 2000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [vals]
